exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a sustained and explicit focus on the prescribed title throughout.
Employs two distinct areas of knowledge with multiple, specific, and relevant examples (X-rays, AI bias, BCI music, Dickinsonâs drafts).
Arguments are generally clear, coherent and demonstrate awareness of alternative perspectives.
weaknesses: The explicit knowledge question is never clearly formulated in the introduction or conclusion.
Evaluation of counter-arguments and underlying assumptions remains brief and asserted rather than deeply critiqued.
Organization and clarity suffer from occasional incomplete paragraphs, implicit transitions, and a conclusion that fails to restate the KQ.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: In reverse-grading from the official total of 6, I allocated marks to the four gradable criteria to reflect relative performance. Understanding of knowledge questions (Criterion A) is strongâclear focus and links to the titleâbut lacks an explicit KQ statement (2/ ?). Quality of analysis (Criterion B) shows coherent argumentation but only superficial evaluation of counter-views (1/ ?). Use of examples (Criterion C) is a particular strength, with well-chosen, specific illustrations across both areas of knowledge (2/ ?). Organization and clarity (Criterion D) are adequate but marred by structural lapses and incomplete transitions (1/ ?). The sum of these criterion scores equals the official total of 6, proportionally reflecting the essayâs strengths in focus and examples alongside its evaluative and structural limitations.

annotations.comment: The historical X-ray example is well-chosen and concrete, illustrating how tool improvement can lead to unanticipated advances in both knowledge and safety protocols.

The discussion of Roentgenâs experiments would benefit from critical evaluation of the ethical and methodological contextâwhy did early scientists proceed without safety standards, and how does this affect our understanding of âimproved knowledgeâ?
The claim of democratization overlooks the potential dilution of expertise or quality. The student could explore how quantity of creators versus depth of skill impacts collective artistic knowledge.
The student effectively specifies the prescribed title verbatim, demonstrating clear awareness of the questionâs focus and establishing a solid foundation for the essay.

The opening paragraph lacks an explicit formulation of a knowledge question. The student should articulate a precise KQâfor example: âTo what extent do improved tools lead to improved knowledge?ââto sharpen the essayâs analytical focus.
The term âdisimproved knowledgeâ is introduced without definition. The student should clarify this concept and explain how it differs from simply âlimitedâ or âerroneousâ knowledge.
The generalisation âIt is human natureâ is asserted without support. The student could strengthen the introduction by referencing a theoretical framework or an example that grounds this claim.

The transition into the natural sciences defines improved knowledge but does not explicitly link back to the KQ parameters (scope, methods, assumptions). The student should connect these terms to their knowledge question.
The distinction between non-accidental (methodological) and accidental (serendipitous) discoveries is clear and shows a sophisticated understanding of how tools function in the natural sciences.

Asserting that radiologists produce more âobjective and verifiableâ knowledge than AI requires evidence or a counter-example. The student should discuss radiologist error rates or decision-making biases to support this claim.
The phrase âhints at regressâ is vague. The student should specify what constitutes a regression in knowledge terms and link it back to the knowledge questionâs criteria.
The paragraph on AI bias uses a relevant contemporary case study and scholarly reference, effectively illustrating how improved tools can introduce systematic errors into scientific knowledge.
While bias is identified, the discussion would be strengthened by analysing potential strategies to mitigate these biases in machine learning and their implications for knowledge claims.
The statement on the irreplaceability of human engagement reads as an assertion. Greater depth could be added by evaluating situations where human judgment also fails, balancing the perspective.

The BCI case would benefit from discussion of limitations (e.g., technological accessibility, user training) to evaluate whether the tool truly improves depth of artistic knowledge or simply broadens participation.
This introductory sentence on the arts remains largely descriptive. The student should present a clear argument about how tools affect the nature of artistic knowledge in epistemic terms.
The definition of artistic knowledge as emotional resonance and shared interpretation is precise and demonstrates understanding of the distinct methods and scope of the arts.
The Brain-Computer Interface example is well-chosen and illustrates a positive case where tool improvement expands access to artistic expression, supporting the argument effectively.
The connection between digital tools and increased accessibility to poetry creation and consumption is well articulated, showing relevance to the knowledge-production process in the arts.

The argument that digital tools detract from reflective writing is asserted without empirical support. The student should cite studies or examples demonstrating decreased creative depth in digital writing.

The use of Emily Dickinsonâs handwritten drafts is a strong illustration of how medium influences the creative process and yields insights into the artistâs thought, enriching the discussion of artistic knowledge.

To deepen the evaluation, the student could consider how digital repositories of Dickinsonâs draft revisions also offer new insights, challenging the claim that digital tools inherently reduce nuance.
The concluding fragment at the bottom of this page is incomplete. The student should revise to ensure each paragraph is fully developed before moving on.

The conclusion effectively synthesizes the key insights from both areas of knowledge, reinforcing the main argument about the non-linear relationship between tool improvement and knowledge enhancement.
The conclusion does not restate the explicit knowledge question. The student should reintroduce the KQ to demonstrate that the essay has answered it fully.

The final recommendation for âcultivating human discernmentâ is interesting but would benefit from specific examples or strategies (e.g., critical-thinking training, interdisciplinary collaboration).

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
